raw_result.description: Work management platform with APIs for managing sheets, rows, reports, dashboards, attachments, users, and related resources.
raw_result.auth_notes: Smartsheet documents two API authentication methods on its developer docs: Direct Access Tokens for user-created API access and 3-legged OAuth 2.0 for apps acting on behalf of users.
raw_result.gating_reason: The Smartsheet API introduction states API access requires a Smartsheet Business, Enterprise, or Advanced Work Management plan.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST APIs are documented for core Smartsheet resources and operations including sheets, rows, reports, dashboards, attachments, users, automation-related resources, plus separate documented APIs such as Resource Management.
api_surface.mcp_notes: The developer introduction page indicates an official Smartsheet MCP server is available.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: The limitations page supports functional API limits such as maximum rows returned or written per request and sheet/cell-related constraints. A precise general requests-per-minute limit was not verified from the gathered page text.
raw_result.notable_signals: Developer docs provide a canonical API introduction, authentication guide, limitations guide, SDK references, and explicit mention of an MCP server, which are strong signs of agent-toolkit readiness.

result.description: Work management platform with APIs for managing sheets, rows, reports, dashboards, attachments, users, and related resources.
result.auth_notes: Smartsheet documents two API authentication methods on its developer docs: Direct Access Tokens for user-created API access and 3-legged OAuth 2.0 for apps acting on behalf of users.
result.gating_reason: The Smartsheet API introduction states API access requires a Smartsheet Business, Enterprise, or Advanced Work Management plan.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST APIs are documented for core Smartsheet resources and operations including sheets, rows, reports, dashboards, attachments, users, automation-related resources, plus separate documented APIs such as Resource Management.
api_surface.mcp_notes: The developer introduction page indicates an official Smartsheet MCP server is available.

result.rate_limit_notes: The limitations page supports functional API limits such as maximum rows returned or written per request and sheet/cell-related constraints. A precise general requests-per-minute limit was not verified from the gathered page text.
result.notable_signals: Developer docs provide a canonical API introduction, authentication guide, limitations guide, SDK references, and explicit mention of an MCP server, which are strong signs of agent-toolkit readiness.
